intro: Steris atropurpurea, also known as Purple Foxglove or Atropa, is a plant species within the Solanaceae family, characterized by its tall, biennial or perennial growth habit and distinctive purple flowers. The health benefits of Steris atropurpurea include its potential to act as a natural antispasmodic and sedative, making it useful for treating anxiety, insomnia, and muscle spasms. The bioactive constituents of this plant include alkaloids such as scopolamine and hyoscyamine, as well as glycosides and flavonoids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, Steris atropurpurea is commonly prepared as a tincture or infusion, often used to treat conditions such as Parkinson's disease, epilepsy, and motion sickness.
